ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-30-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hengameh Kishani, via ZOOM, for Steven Sum, Petitioner; Matthew Kloenhamer, via ZOOM, for David Sum and Charlene Sum, Objectors.

Ms. Kishani reports that the matter settled last week and that the parties have a signed agreement and are working on the terms to finalize it. The court then asks if the parties plan on filing a petition to approve the settlement agreement. Ms. Kishani advises that she doesn't believe so and that she anticipates filing a Dismissal within the next few weeks. Mr. Kloenhamer also advises that he anticipates filing a Dismissal within the next few weeks

The Case Management Hearing is off calendar. The Petitions are continued to Dec-28-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on the Filing of Dismissals is added for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hengameh Kishani appeared by zoom for Steven Sum (Petitioner).
Jeanne Grove and Morgan Cahill-Marsland appeared by zoom for and with Charlene Sum by zoom and for David Sum (Respondents).

The Court stated that it received the joint case management statement ("CMS"), that it appreciates the detail that went into it and that based on what it read in the CMS, it is going to refer the matter to pro bono mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 10, 2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 16, 2021. The Petition to Invalidate Trust for Lack of Capacity and Undue Influence, and the Petition for Elder Abuse Against Petitioner Steven Sum are continued to and the Parties are to return on November 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to report on status of mediation. The order referring the case to pro bono mediation was signed in open Court. The parties are to obtain a copy of the mediation order through the Court's website.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Trust For Lack Of Capcity And Undue Influence; For Financial Elder Abuse; Ad For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Continued To Aug-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hengameh Kishani appeared by zoom for Steven Sum (Petitioner).
Jeanne Grove and Morgan Cahill-Marsland appeared by zoom for David Sum and Charlene Sum (Respondents/Cross-Petitioners).

The Court stated that it received the joint case management statement, that it was very helpful and thanked the Parties. The Court also noted that the report raises the issue of Steven's intent to file a motion to compel, informed Counsel that the motions need to be question and party specific, and asked if there have been any further developments. Ms. Kishani stated that she received responses on Friday, that hopefully this will resolve the meet and confer effort on written discovery, and that the next step is to conduct depositions. The Court set case management hearings for August 24, 2021 and November 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court also set a trial date of February 22, 2022 at 9;00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the purpose of determining whether the trial will remain in the Probate Department, or be sent out to the trial department. The Court informed Counsel that witnesses will not be needed on that date and that it will issue a case management order. The Parties are to monitor the Court's register of actions and Steven Sum is directed to obtain a copy of the order and serve it on all parties. The Court also informed Counsel that it will set a mediation schedule at the next calling of the case. The Petition to Invalidate Trust for Lack of Capacity and Undue Influence, etc., and the Petition for Elder Abuse against Steven Sum are continued to August 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Text Ruling

Respondents Petition For Elder Abuse Against Petitioner Steven Sum

Respondents Petition For Elder Abuse Against Petitioner Steven Sum
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Continued To May-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Invalidate Trust For Lack Of Capcity And Undue Influence; For Financial Elder Abuse; Ad For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Continued To May-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
James Cilley for and with Steve Sum, Petitioner/Cross-Respondent.
Jeanne Grove and Morgan Cahill-Marsland for and with David Sum and Charlene Sum, Respondents/Cross-Petitioners.

Mr. Cilley stated parties need to take depositions and complete discovery, that his preference is for matters to be heard and tried together as they arise from same operative facts and whether the matters are monitored in probate or civil division they will end up in the same place. Ms. Grove stated the elder abuse claim belongs in civil, that matters should be tried together and leaves it up to the court to decide if it wants to transfer the matter to the civil trial department to complete discovery. The Court asked Ms. Grove why she didn't file in civil if she says it belongs in civil. Ms. Grove stated she filed as a cross-complaint to Steven's petition, that both sides anticipate multiple witnesses and expect the case to be a long cause. Mr. Cilley stated Steven's petition was filed before the probate court with proper jurisdiction and that both matters should be consolidated and tried together. The court noted that none of the contingent beneficiaries have been noticed. Ms. Grove stated she was just added to the case, that her office did not do the notice, that she didn't look into it, but believes her clients did satisfy the notice requirements. The court stated there are statutory/constitutional notice requirements, and counsel should look into it to make sure the notice requirements are met. The Court further stated that it will issue case management orders on these petitions, that it will not get into dates at this point, possibly address dates in the next case management order, that parties are required to meet and confer and submit a joint case management statement. All parties are advised to monitor the court's register of actions within the next couple of days for the filed case management order to be uploaded and counsel for Steven Sum is to serve the order when it is available.

The Petition to invalidate trust for lack of capacity and undue influence, etc. and the Petition for elder abuse against Steven Sum are continued to May 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Court Reporter: David Salyer, CSR#4410, (323-497-1441; davesal55@gmail.com).

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum V. Kaiser Permanente Antioch Medical
Off Calendar Jan-20-2021 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum V. Kaiser Permanente Antioch Medical
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Continued To Jan-20-21 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marrianne Taleghani, Esq. appearing by Zoom for Respondents Charlene and David Sum.
Ashley Klein, Esq. appearing by Zoom for Respondents Charlene and David Sum.
James P. Cilley, Esq. appearing by Zoom for Petitioner Steven Sum.

The matter was called. The Court stated that it had received the CCP 170.6 challenge. Ms. Taleghani stated that they wished to assert their right.

At approximately 2:10 p.m. a fire alarm at the courthouse interrupted these proceedings. The parties remained on Zoom while the Judge and court staff in the courtroom exited the building.

At approximately 2:45 p.m. the parties informed the clerk that Court Reporter Julie Park had an appointment at 3:00 p.m., that she had to leave, and that they were in the process of securing another court reporter for the hearing. Off the record, the clerk provides the parties with a case cite from the Court.

At 3:09 p.m. the second call of the matter commences, the Court inquired if the parties wished to recall the matter and begin again since the proceedings were interrupted by a fire alarm in the courthouse. The parties opted to begin again. The matter is called and the Court stated that it had received the CCP 170.6. Ms. Klein presents argument in support of the 170.6. Mr. Cilley presents argument in oppostion to the 170.6. Argument concludes, and the CCP 170.6 challenge as to Judge Ross Moody stands. The matter is continued to January 20, 2021 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204 before the Hon. Joseph M. Quinn.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter at the initial call of the matter: Julie Park, CSR# 13925, Tel. 213.222.7024. Court Reporter at the second call of the matter after the fire alarm: Sheila Pham, CSR# 13293, Tel. 415.274.9977, Email: calendar-ca@veritext.com.

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Enforce Subpoena Duces Tecum V City And County Of San Francisco Human Services Agency, Adult Protective Services Division
Off Calendar Dec-03-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Morgan Cahill-Marsland, Esq. appearing by Zoom for Respondents David Sum and Charlene Sum.
James Ries, Esq., Dep. City Attorney, appearing by Zoom for non-party San Francisco Human Services Agency.
Hengameh Kishani, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for Petitioner Steven Sum.

Matter called. Argument on Respondents Charlene Sum and David Sum's Notice of Motion and Motion to Enforce Subpoena Duces Tecum V. City and County of San Francisco Human Services Agency, Adult Protective Services Division is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Respondents' motion to compel is denied. Welfare and Institutions Code section 15633 provides that reports of elder abuse are confidential and may not be disclosed, except to persons to whom disclosure is permitted under Welfare and Institutions Code section 15633.5 or members of multidisciplinary teams concerned with the prevention, identification, or treatment of elder abuse. These confidentiality protections are designed to preserve the privacy interests of both alleged abusers and alleged victims of abuse, and to encourage candid reporting of suspected abuse or neglect. (See Chronicle Pub. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1960) 54 Cal.2d 548, 569-570 and Easton v. Sutter Coast Hosp. (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 485, 491.)
Respondents contend that any "report from APS is a crucial piece of evidence to show that Petitioner abused his elderly mother." (Respondents' MPA ISO Motion to Enforce Subpoena, p.4.) They argue that they will be "significantly disadvantaged" without release of APS files. (Ibid.) Finally, they contend that "release of the requested documents" is "proper under Welfare & Institutions Code sections 15633(b)(1) and 15633.5(c)(2)." (Id., p.3.) Respondents arguments are not persuasive. First, the fact that respondents believe that it would be helpful to their case to receive confidential APS files is not a reason to override the clear confidentiality provisions established by the Legislature by enacting sections 15633 and 15633.5. The statutes explicitly require confidentiality and clearly restrict access to APS files to the entities listed in section 15633.5. Respondents, as private litigants in a trust matter, do not qualify to receive confidential APS files under any of the provisions of section 15633.5. The Court is not prepared to expand the list of individuals and entities entitled to access to the confidential files. (Adoption of Kelsey S. (1992) 1 Cal.4th 816, 826-827 [the primary role of the court " 'in construing a statute is to determine the Legislature's intent' "; "[i]f the Legislature has provided an express definition, we must take it as we find it" and may not "violate the cardinal rule that courts may not add provisions to a statute"].)

Moreover, Respondents reliance on sections 15633(b)(1) and 15633.5(c)(2) is misplaced. The most those sections authorize, when read together, would be disclosure of the "identity of a person who reports" abuse to the agency in cases where "a person reporting waives confidentiality," not the broad documents discovery sought here. (See Welf. & Inst. Code sections 15633(b)(1) and 15633.5(c)(2). These sections provide no basis for the relief sought.

Reliance on Welfare and Institutions Code section 10850(a) is equally misplaced. That section applies by its terms to "any provision of this code relating to any form of public social services," not APS reports. Moreover, the reference to "civil proceeding" plainly does not apply here, as this is not a "civil proceeding conducted in connection with the administration of [a public social services] program" but rather is a private dispute over a trust.

The Court directed Mr. Ries to prepare a form of order and circulate it to counsel. Mr. Ries informed the Court that an order was dropped off at the clerk's office today.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: R. Rosado. Matter not reported.
